label: Geneva — UN Mediation Hub
sub: Headquarters of OHCHR, UNHCR, UNOG, and dozens of peace processes including Syria, Yemen, Libya consultations. Annual Human Rights Council sessions.

label: Oslo — Oslo Accords (1993)
sub: Secret back-channel talks hosted by Norway's FAFO Institute led to the Israel–PLO Declaration of Principles signed Sep 13, 1993. Norway continues as peace mediator in Colombia (ELN/FARC) and other contexts.

label: Doha — Peace Talks Hub
sub: Host of U.S.–Taliban Doha Agreement (Feb 2020), Gaza ceasefire negotiations (2023–26), Sudan peace consultations, and Afghan intra-dialogue. Qatar's mediation role is central to multiple active processes.

label: Belfast — Good Friday Agreement (1998)
sub: The Belfast Agreement of April 10, 1998 ended 30 years of 'The Troubles'. Northern Ireland's power-sharing institutions remain the model for consociational peace. Brexit tensions test the agreement's Irish dimension.

label: Dayton — Bosnia Peace Accords (1995)
sub: The General Framework Agreement for Peace in Bosnia was initialed at Wright-Patterson AFB on Nov 21, 1995, ending the Bosnian War. Created Bosnia's two-entity structure under a unified state. NATO IFOR/SFOR enforced peace.

label: Bogotá — Colombia FARC Peace (2016)
sub: Final peace agreement with FARC-EP signed Nov 24, 2016 after 4 years of Havana negotiations. JEP (Special Jurisdiction for Peace) operates here. Total Peace policy of Petro government includes ELN talks, now suspended 15+ months after January 2025 Catatumbo massacre.

label: Havana — Colombia Peace Talks Venue
sub: Cuba hosted FARC–government talks 2012–2016 and continues as guarantor for ELN negotiations under Petro's Total Peace framework. Joint guarantor with Norway. ELN talks suspended since January 2025 Catatumbo massacre.

label: Addis Ababa — African Union HQ
sub: African Union Commission headquarters. Hub for IGAD, AUHIP (Sudan), AU peace missions. Hosted Ethiopia–Tigray Pretoria Agreement (2022) implementation monitoring. Key venue for Horn of Africa peace diplomacy.

label: Nairobi — IGAD / EAC Peace Hub
sub: Hosted Sudan CPA signing (2005), South Sudan R-ARCSS follow-on talks, and DRC–Rwanda Nairobi Track negotiations. IGAD headquarters. Kenya's Uhuru Kenyatta mediated Ethiopia–Tigray Pretoria process. RSF's Hemeti in Apr 2026 Nairobi talks with UN envoy Haavisto.

label: Pretoria — Ethiopia–Tigray Agreement (2022)
sub: Cessation of Hostilities Agreement signed Nov 2, 2022, ending 2 years of devastating war. AU-mediated by Obasanjo, Kenyatta, Mlambo-Ngcuka. Most significant African peace deal of the 2020s.

label: Jeddah — Sudan Peace Platform
sub: U.S.–Saudi Arabia jointly host Jeddah talks between SAF and RSF since May 2023. Multiple ceasefire declarations signed here have collapsed within hours. US Adviser Boulos presented five-pillar peace roadmap on Sudan's 3-year anniversary, April 15, 2026.

label: Stockholm — Yemen Hodeidah Agreement (2018)
sub: UN-brokered Stockholm Agreement of Dec 13, 2018 includes Hodeidah port ceasefire and prisoner exchange framework. UN Mission to support the Hodeidah Agreement (UNMHA) monitors ceasefire. Prevented catastrophic battle for Yemen's main supply port.

label: Minsk — Ukraine–Russia (Minsk I & II)
sub: Minsk Protocol (Sep 2014) and Minsk II (Feb 2015) established ceasefire and political roadmap for Donbas. OSCE monitored compliance. Russia's 2022 full invasion rendered the Minsk framework defunct. Both parties dispute who negotiated in bad faith.

label: Kyiv — Russia–Ukraine War Front
sub: Russia's Feb 24, 2022 invasion. Brief Istanbul talks (March 2022) collapsed. U.S.-Russia tracks exclude Kyiv; Zelensky demands inclusion (Apr 2026). Easter POW exchange Apr 11 (175 soldiers + 7 civilians) via UAE facilitation offers humanitarian progress.

label: Gaza — Ceasefire Negotiations
sub: Qatar/Egypt/U.S.-mediated ceasefire negotiations since October 2023. Phase I framework agreed Jan 15, 2025 (42-day pause, hostage releases). Phase II permanent ceasefire disputed. Over 50,000 Palestinian deaths reported; 2M displaced within Gaza.

label: Khartoum — Sudan Civil War
sub: SAF vs RSF conflict erupted Apr 15, 2023. Three-year mark Apr 15, 2026: 150,000+ killed, 14M displaced, famine in Darfur. US imposes sanctions and presents five-pillar peace roadmap. SAF retook capital Mar 2025; RSF controls most of Darfur.

label: Goma — DRC M23/Rwanda Conflict
sub: M23 rebels captured Goma Jan 27, 2025. Washington Accords (Dec 2025) stalled: Rwanda has not withdrawn troops, FDLR not disbanded. UN Security Council briefing Apr 17, 2026 warns of collapse risk. 7M+ displaced in eastern DRC.

label: Cotabato — Bangsamoro Peace (Philippines)
sub: Capital of the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM), established 2019 under the Bangsamoro Organic Law. MILF decommissioning proceeds in phases. BIFF and Abu Sayyaf remain spoilers.

label: Freetown — Sierra Leone Post-Conflict TJ
sub: Special Court for Sierra Leone operated 2002–2013, convicting Charles Taylor (in absentia) and RUF leaders. Truth and Reconciliation Commission completed its work 2004. Sierra Leone recovery model cited as post-conflict success despite ongoing governance challenges.

label: Bogotá — Colombia JEP (Transitional Justice)
sub: Colombia's Special Jurisdiction for Peace (JEP) investigates war crimes by FARC and military. Macro-case 01 examines 6,402+ cases of 'false positives' (civilians killed by army and registered as combatants). Colombia's TJ model is the most advanced in the Americas.

label: Banda Aceh — Helsinki MOU (2005)
sub: Post-tsunami peace breakthrough: Helsinki MOU ended 29-year GAM insurgency. EU and ASEAN monitoring mission verified demobilization. Aceh given special autonomy and oil revenue sharing. Considered a landmark post-disaster peace opportunity.

label: Mogadishu — Somalia Reconciliation
sub: AU Mission in Somalia (AMISOM/ATMIS) and UN Assistance Mission (UNSOM) support federal reconciliation process. Clan-based power sharing (4.5 formula) underpins government formation. Al-Shabaab controls 20%+ of territory. National Consultative Council attempts to resolve federal-state disputes.

label: Tripoli — Libya Peace Process
sub: Government of National Unity (GNU) recognized by UN but contested by Benghazi-based government. Libya Political Dialogue Forum (LPDF) attempts to bridge east-west divide. Ceasefire signed Oct 2020 broadly holds but elections remain blocked since Dec 2021. Turkey and UAE/Egypt back competing factions.

label: Nicosia — Cyprus Reunification Talks
sub: UN-mediated talks on bizonal bicommunal federation have continued since 1974 Turkish intervention. Most recent formal talks at Crans-Montana 2017 collapsed. UN Special Adviser Jane Holl Lute conducts informal consultations. Divided capital remains last divided city in EU-candidate zone.

label: Kathmandu — Nepal Transitional Justice
sub: Nepal's Truth and Reconciliation Commission and Commission on Enforced Disappearances were established under the 2014 TRC Act but stalled repeatedly. A landmark Supreme Court ruling 2024 requires accountability for conflict-era crimes. Nepal's TJ process is one of the world's longest-stalled.

label: Islamabad — US–Iran Nuclear Talks (2026)
sub: Pakistan hosted the first round of direct U.S.-Iran diplomatic talks in April 2026, brokered after a seven-week military conflict. Talks collapsed after 21 hours over uranium enrichment terms. US naval blockade of Iranian ports began April 13. Iran declared no plans for further negotiations on April 20. Pakistan had offered to host a second, multi-day round.

label: Washington DC — DRC–Rwanda Accords (2025)
sub: U.S.-facilitated Washington Accords signed December 4, 2025 by DRC and Rwanda to end M23/eastern Congo conflict. Implementation stalled by April 2026: Rwandan troops remain, M23 holds Goma, FDLR not disbanded. UN Security Council warned of collapse risk on April 17, 2026.
